The fundamental flaw of most social platforms is a tech mindset that thinks the hard part is managing content, when the hard part is actually managing discontent.

How the Musk-Twitter showdown ends. 1. Twitter's board has a duty to max return 2. Twitter, the firm, doesn't want Musk 3. Musk doesn't want Twitter Musk offered $54/share. Current price: $37. Negotiation begins by demanding $17/share for Musk to walk https://theatlantic.com/...

The narrative that Elon is trying to weasel out of buying Twitter because the price went down doesn't fit the data. The price went up after there were hints that he might buy it and went down to its former price after, so it seems the price is following him.
it's like the court schedule is on autopilot  https://twitter.com/...
He seems determined to ruin the company https://twitter.com/...

From filing: “With the sense of humor of a bot, Twitter claims that Musk is damaging the company with tweets like a Chuck Norris meme and a poop emoji. Twitter ignores that Musk is its second largest shareholder with a far greater economic stake than the entire Twitter board.”
Second Friday night bomb in a row from the Musk legal team
Oh *they're* the ones acting in bad faith. Riiiiiiiiight. https://twitter.com/...

Judge Kathaleen McCormick will hear arguments on July 19 for Twitter's request for a September trial in its lawsuit seeking to hold Elon Musk, the world's richest person, to his $44 billion deal for the social media platform, according to a court filing https://www.reuters.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Reminder that Twitter asked for things to be expedited, hoping to wrap this all up by late October (would have been ambitious regardless I think...) https://twitter.com/...
When you're holding an out of the money option, it always makes sense to try to extend the expiry date.
SEC filing: Twitter's board invites shareholders to a special meeting to vote on Elon Musk's acquisition and unanimously recommends they approve the deal
To the Stockholders of Twitter, Inc.
Twitter wants shareholders to vote for the Musk deal despite the lawsuit
Elon Musk responds to ‘meritless’ Twitter suit, says he wants to push trial back to 2023

5. This case was randomly assigned to to Chancellor McCormick, who in a similar case last year, ordered a buyer to go through with a merger even after COVID and the torpedoing of the deal's financing. Buyer was forced to go through with the acquisition! https://www.businesswire.com/ ...

4. Musk's final claim is that Twitter stopped operating in the “ordinary course” of business. I talked to Brian Quinn, a law professor at Boston College, about this. He said “ordinary course” claims have held up in the Delaware Court of Chancery. This could be Musk's best shot! https://twitter.com/...

4 cont.: ... There's just one problem. Twitter says it repeatedly reached out to Musk and his representatives about an employee retention program. Musk didn't seem to care. At the town hall, Musk was noncommittal about ppl leaving, since costs needed to come down. https://twitter.com/...

Elon Musk is in deep trouble here, for 5 reasons. 1. He's already arguably violated the disparagement clause in Section 6.8 of the contract by criticizing Twitter (see: poop tweets, etc). It's hard to avail yourself of a contract's protections if you're already in violation. https://twitter.com/...

In my younger days (uh, April), I thought a Twitter power-user would be a fun Twitter steward. Welp. Market crashed, and Musk changed his mind. Today, his power tweets are an artillery of petards by which Twitter may hoist him in court. He's in trouble. https://www.theatlantic.com/ ...

3. As many ppl have pointed out, Musk's second claim—that Twitter won't give him sufficient information about bots—is a little more plausible. But Twitter's response is compelling. Weeks after the agreement was inked, and they still sent over massive amounts of customer data. https://twitter.com/...

2. As many people have pointed out, Musk's primary claim that he can't acquire a company with such a huge bots problem is tough to make, given his stated preference to buy Twitter *so that he can clean up the bots* https://twitter.com/...

FCC Chair Jessica Rosenworcel proposes a US broadband standard of 100Mbps down and 20Mbps up; the proposal requires a vote in the currently deadlocked FCC
Chairwoman Rosenworcel Proposes to Increase Minimum Broadband Speeds
FCC chair proposes raising broadband standard to 100Mbps
FCC Looks to Raise Minimum Broadband Speed Metric From 25Mbps to 100Mbps
FCC Chair Wants Faster FCC Broadband Speed Definition
Minimum broadband speeds should rise to 100/20, FCC chair proposes

Chairwoman @JRosenworcelFCC has proposed several benchmarks that will increase the national minimum #broadband speeds and set a higher gigabit goal for the future. Details here: https://www.fcc.gov/.... #FCCGov #BroadbandForAll

The 25/3 metric isn't just behind the times, it's harmful because it masks the extent to which rural and low-income neighborhoods are being left behind and offline. We must raise the standard for minimum broadband speeds now and aim higher for the future. https://www.fcc.gov/...
Changing this definition is long-past due, but also going to face an uphill battle from industry groups https://arstechnica.com/...
good move by @JRosenworcelFCC and long overdue. Pathetic speed standards have, for decades, helped obscure the impact of monopolization and consolidation. https://arstechnica.com/...

SCOOP: The mystery debtor that owes Celsius $439m is EquitiesFirst, an Indianapolis-based specialist lender Best known for lending against stock, EquitiesFirst failed to return crypto Celsius had pledged for a loan in 2021, acc to court filing https://www.ft.com/...

We may be hearing more about EquitiesFirst. Earlier this week, EquitiesFirst made an appearance as a creditor in the US bankruptcy proceedings for Three Arrows Capital... and then quickly withdrew https://twitter.com/...

We have always prioritized client's interest through transparency. To date, EquitiesFirst has returned over US$343,600,000 to Celsius Network since Sept last year based on contractual agreements. Both parties are in ongoing negotiations working toward mutually beneficial goals.

Celsius referenced a debt owed by a “private lending platform” in a court filing yday. Two sources confirmed it was EquitiesFirst. EquitiesFirst said it is in “ongoing conversation with our client and both parties have agreed to extend our obligations” https://twitter.com/...
I was so curious and never would have guessed https://twitter.com/...
This could be huge - first tradfi institution caught up in the crypto crash. https://twitter.com/...

“A lawyer for EquitiesFirst on Tuesday joined a separate US crypto bankruptcy case, that of failed hedge fund Three Arrows Capital. The filing said EquitiesFirst was a creditor. Hours later, the lawyer filed to withdraw.” https://www.ft.com/...
EquitiesFirst is such an awesome Indiana shadow bank name https://twitter.com/...
Terrific scoop. Also a fun one for those who remember Quindell, which had some controversial dealings with EquitiesFirst https://www.ft.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Oh this is hilarious: EquitiesFirst specializes in asset backed lending. So, e.g., Musk has billions in Tesla stock but he doesn't want to sell, both because it would tank the market and cost him taxes. So he borrows against it instead... https://www.ft.com/...

According to internal documents, Celsius execs sold over $40 million $CEL back to the company in 2020-21. That's in addition to the ~$40 mil Mashinsky dumped directly onto the market! @Bitfinexed @SilvermanJacob @concodanomics @BennettTomlin https://www.ft.com/... https://twitter.com/...
it must have been frustrating being on the compliance team. https://www.ft.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Quite the read by @kadhim and Joshua Oliver via @FT ↙️ Inside #Celsius: How one of #crypto's biggest lenders ground to a halt https://www.ft.com/... “Former employees & internal documents suggest a reckless pursuit of high returns.”
Great story on Celsius, and how its founders sold millions before the company crashed https://www.ft.com/...

Pharos Fund belongs to Lantern Ventures, which had an estimated $400m under management as of an April filing. in turn, Lantern Ventures is staffed by a host of people with links to SBF, whether that's at his companies or at a charity he favours. https://twitter.com/...

I still remember having to explain why I wanted to go work on Linux as a young kid. Recruiters told me it was worthless. Old greybeard techs said it wasn't good enough. Fast forward to today and Linux runs the world. https://www.technologyreview.com/ ...

1/ Mixers are receiving more #crypto than ever in 2022. The 30-day moving avg reached an all-time high of $51.8M worth of crypto on 4/19/22, roughly doubling incoming volumes YoY. Let's break down the increase in mixer usage & where those funds are going. https://blog.chainalysis.com/ ... https://twitter.com/...

If 23% of the money brought in by any other business (or 12% for that matter) was known to be illicit, it would be regulated out of business. And remember: these numbers only account for the *known* illicit transactions. The real numbers are far higher. https://twitter.com/...

Chainalysis: llicit addresses account for 23% of funds sent to mixers so far in 2022. Nearly all of the remaining funds moving from sanctioned entities to mixers come from two groups associated with the North Korean government: Lazarus and Blender. https://blog.chainalysis.com/ ...

BREAKING: Disney trying to make money like every other company on the planet. https://twitter.com/...
Madness. Subscription fatigue is going to be the failure of a LOT of these streaming services. https://twitter.com/...
Notable news ahead of college football season. Some SEC and Big 12 games, along with a lot of G5, are on ESPN+. https://twitter.com/...

All those millions of $$$$s being paid to big-time college football/basketball factories are quickly being passed on to their fans who watch on ESPN. And it's about to go sky high. Unboxed Nothing Phone (1). Surprisingly the battery was absolutely dead out of the box 0%. Here's Nothing 45W charger as well the Transparent case for the phone. No case is included in the box. You need to purchase it separately. Same for the charger. Watch the thread for more https://twitter.com/...

ICYMI, today Facebook/Meta released snippets of its long awaited human rights impact assessment on India in its first human rights report, which a) brings us no closer understanding the company's role in human rights abuses or what commitments it will make to address the issue &

“The 83-page report, covering the years 2020 and 2021, strikes a largely self-congratulatory tone, defending Meta's misinformation strategy while failing to touch on allegations of biased content moderation.” https://techcrunch.com/...
A business-as-usual scenario without effective content moderation on Meta's platform such as Facebook has serious human rights implications for Indian minorities, and for India as a democracy.

The report says the independent auditor made “recommendations” about what Facebook should do to improve its human rights impact in India. But Facebook won't say what those reccos were. And then they spend nearly a page saying what they're already doing.

The report summarizes the “risks” identified by the auditor in two short paragraphs. But doesn't elaborate. To be clear - this report (released by Facebook) is a summary of an unreleased, and presumably much more detailed, impact assessment carried out by law firm Foley Hoag

This is the company saying that “informed consent” from people being recorded by the devices is an issue. But it also makes no proposals on how to correct that beyond doing “user research” and some jargon about exploring “other signals for bystanders”.

I'm happy to be convinced otherwise, but perhaps there is just no way to obtain true informed consent from bystanders for a recording device built to look like glasses? Having a red dot that turns on while recording is not informed consent.

And we call again for independent, external oversight and regulation of Facebook. Without it, a self-reporting, self-regulating Meta will continue to violate human rights with impunity and share only the details they like.

It's not often I get to be part of a transformative moment, but today we released Meta's First Annual Human Rights Report. In this report, @Meta is taking a major step to uphold human rights principles. We still have much work to do, but it's been an honor to work on this. https://twitter.com/...

New: Meta said last year they'd start releasing annual reports on how its services affect human rights globally. The first is out. They detail efforts to combat human trafficking, misinfo, attacks on election integrity and more. Story with @sal19: https://www.wsj.com/...

Meta strives to apply human rights principles to every facet of our business. We are proud of the progress documented in this report and recognize there's more work to be done. https://about.fb.com/... https://twitter.com/...

This report shares a lot of the amazing work of the human rights team at Meta. It includes lot of detailed insights and actions from Meta's human rights due diligence on products, countries and responses to emerging crises. https://about.fb.com/...

Today's report represents a detailed look at the impact of that work - from fighting harmful misinformation and ensuring AI governance is rooted in democratic values - and the steps we're taking to learn, improve and hold ourselves accountable. https://about.fb.com/...
